title: Could Sarah Strong be UConn's all-time best -- and a four-time champ?
content: Strong had a freshman season for the record books -- and then she got better. Is another fairy-tale ending on the way?

title: Tide's Holloway to enroll in fall, aims to play again
content: Following Friday's reversal of his campus ban after his recent arrest, Alabama's Aden Holloway is planning to enroll in fall classes and hoping to rejoin the team next season pending the outcome of his legal case, his attorney told ESPN.

title: Boozer fifth freshman to win AP Player of Year
content: Cameron Boozer is The Associated Press men's college basketball national Player of the Year, becoming only the fifth freshman to earn the honor and the second in a row for a Duke program that keeps adding to the longest list of winners in the country.

title: UConn's Hurley owns up to antics: 'Not a victim'
content: UConn coach Dan Hurley says he is not looking for any sympathy regarding the perception of his coaching style.

title: Nebraska's Hoiberg named AP Coach of the Year
content: Nebraska's Fred Hoiberg edged out Duke's Jon Scheyer and Arizona's Tommy Lloyd for Associated Press men's basketball coach of the year honors Friday.

title: Royals' Jensen oversleeps, scratched from lineup
content: Catcher Carter Jensen was removed from the Royals' starting lineup for Thursday's game against the Twins after he overslept.

title: Tiger told deputy: 'Was just talking to president'
content: After crashing his SUV last week in Florida, Tiger Woods took out his phone and told a deputy, "I was just talking to the president," according to body camera footage showing the golfer's arrest on a DUI charge.

title: Wilson committed to Aces: 'I'm not leaving Vegas'
content: A'ja Wilson, four-time MVP and three-time WNBA champion with the Aces, said she is "not leaving Vegas" when it comes time to sign a new deal.

title: ⚽ The top men's U21 soccer players in the world
content: For the eighth straight year, ESPN brings you our list of the game's next young stars aged 21 or under. Who is good enough to make it?

title: The incredibly rare thing you probably missed during the Cubs' 2016 curse-breaking World Series win
content: While the Cubs were making history in Cleveland 10 years ago, one unique accomplishment flew well under the radar.

title: Final NBA MVP (and ROY) straw poll: Here's who voters are picking to win
content: Can Wemby's late push unseat SGA? Tim Bontemps polls 100 NBA insiders for the latest MVP snapshot. Plus, a look at the race for top rookie.

title: Latest NFL draft buzz: What we're hearing on Jermod McCoy's pro day, the QB3 debate
content: How did the Tennessee CB perform at his pro day? Who is the third-best QB in this class? Our draft experts weigh in with new intel three weeks out.

title: Safety 'savant' Caleb Downs to test NFL's thirst for his position
content: Since 2000, only eight safeties have been drafted in the top 10. Downs could challenge conventional wisdom.

title: 'We want to pinch ourselves': Dusty May's full-circle return to the Final Four
content: Three years after helming FAU's Cinderella run to the national semifinals, May returns with Michigan.

title: Your men's Final Four guide: Illinois-UConn, Arizona-Michigan (with predictions!)
content: We run through every team's keys to advancing to the championship game -- and their potential fatal flaws.

title: NHL playoff watch: The Flyers' path to the postseason
content: Philly needs a win Friday -- and some help, too. Plus, full updated playoff projections after a 14-game night.

title: Weekend predictions: Man City or Liverpool in FA Cup? Can Atlético play spoiler vs. Barça?
content: From a big-club clash in England to a potentially decisive LaLiga matchup, another wild weekend of action awaits as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

title: Premier League without set-piece goals: Arsenal still first?
content: We crunched the numbers on how the standings would look like without goals from corners, free kicks and more.

title: Scouts debate the top of the NBA draft class: Peterson or Dybantsa at No. 1?
content: Fit over talent? Versatility over production? Scouts and execs break down the conversations happening.

title: UConn on brink of dual title appearance brings 2014 to mind
content: UConn men's and women's basketball are both in the Final Four. Could the school make its first dual national championship appearance since 2014? The world was quite different back then.

title: Tracking fifth-year options for 2023 first-rounders: Who will return in 2027?
content: The deadline for teams to pick up the fifth-year option on rookie contracts is May 1. Which players have had their options picked up?

title: Men's turnament picks: How to bet Saturday's national semifinal games
content: Will the Illini get revenge for the loss to UConn back in November? Is the total too high in the late game?

title: Alvarez a top-10 bat? Ohtani wins Cy Young? Perdomo for real? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

title: Keepers, sleepers and more: Top 10 notes for next season
content: As the NHL season nears its end, here are the keepers, sleepers and bouncebacks to know for next season.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Four Premier League clubs want Villa star Rogers
content: Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal, and Liverpool are interested in Aston Villa midfielder Morgan Rogers. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Pak-Led Ceasefire Efforts Collapse As Iran Rejects 'Unacceptable' US Demands: Report
content: The push, led by Pakistan, has failed to yield a breakthrough, with Tehran formally notifying mediators that it is unwilling to send officials to Islamabad for talks in the coming days.

title: Pak-Led Ceasefire Efforts Collapse As Iran Rejects 'Unacceptable' US Demands: Report
content: The push, led by Pakistan, has failed to yield a breakthrough, with Tehran formally notifying mediators that it is unwilling to send officials to Islamabad for talks in the coming days.

title: Donald Trump Names JD Vance "Fraud Czar". What That Means
content: Trump said that the focus of his work will be "everywhere" but primarily in Democratic-run states, singling out California, Illinois and Minnesota.

title: 'Capture Enemy Pilots Alive': Iran Channel Offers Reward After US Jet Downed
content: An Iranian channel announced a reward for civilians if the pilots were captured and handed over to the police and military forces.

title: UP Man Kills 9-Month-Old Son With Axe During Argument With Wife: Cops
content: A man allegedly killed his nine-month-old son with an axe here on Friday, police said, adding that the accused has been arrested.

title: NATO Chief To Visit US Next Week Amid Trump's Threat To Quit Alliance
content: Trump has suggested he is considering quitting the 77-year-old military alliance due to the response by European nations to his war.

title: FIR Doesn't Say 'Honourable' Minister, Court Asks Top Official To Explain Why
content: The Allahabad High Court has sought an explanation from the additional chief secretary (home) of Uttar Pradesh over the non-mentioning of the courtesy title 'Hon'ble' before the name of a Union minister in an FIR.

title: NDTV Ground Report: Israeli Strikes In Lebanon Takes Death Count Past 1,300
content: Israel carried out a fresh wave of attacks in Lebanon on Friday, killing four people, including two worshippers, according to local authorities.

title: US Forces Have Rescued 1 of 2 Crew Members Of Downed Warplane In Iran: Report
content: US Iran War F15 Fighter Jet: US Forces Have Rescued 1 of 2 Crew Members Of Downed Warplane In Iran: Report

title: Rs 13,90,17,07,50,00,000: Amount White House Wants For Defence Amid Iran War
content: The proposal would lift Pentagon spending by more than 40 percent in a single year -- the sharpest increase since World War II -- as Washington seeks to sustain military operations and rebuild depleted weapons stockpiles.

title: Satellite Pic Shows Damage At Iran's "Toll Collector" Base Hit By Airstrikes
content: An analysis of the image by NDTV Datafy suggests fires at four warehouses scattered across the waterfront infrastructure and support buildings at the base.

title: Trump Mocks Starmer Over Help In Iran, UK's "Old, Broken" Aircraft Carriers
content: During an Easter lunch speech at the White House, Trump claimed that Starmer said he would have to speak to his team before deciding to send over UK aircraft carriers for the US-Israeli war against Iran.

title: 20-Year-Old Stabbed To Death After Dispute In Delhi: Cops
content: A 20-year-old man was stabbed to death following a quarrel near Haiderpur Nahar in northwest Delhi, police said on Friday.

title: Adani Ports Targets 1 Billion Tonnes Cargo By 2030 After 500 Million Milestone
content: The milestone marks a significant step in the ports-to-energy conglomerate's ambition to scale up infrastructure capacity in line with India's rising trade and industrial demand.

title: Zohran Mamdani To Take On New York's 'Bad Landlords' At Rental Hearings
content: Mamdani, a democratic socialist swept into office on a promise of zealous tenant advocacy, framed the Bronx event as a struggle session for renters.

title: 'Can anyone find our pilots? Please?': Iran ridicules US after American jet hit in Iran
content: Iran's Parliament Speaker mocked the US after an F-15E fighter jet crashed over Iran, questioning the Pentagon's claims of air superiority. The fate of the two-member crew is unknown, with US teams reportedly scrambling for a rescue. This incident follows a series of US aircraft losses amid escalating regional tensions and threats against Iran.

title: IPL: Iyer-led PBKS hunt down 210 with ease, hand CSK second straight loss
content: Punjab Kings beat Chennai Super Kings by five wickets, chasing 210 with ease to register their second straight win at the MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai. CSK batter Ayush Mhatre starred with 73 as CSK posted 209/5. In reply, Shreyas Iyer’s 50 and strong support helped PBKS complete the chase comfortably in 18.4 overs.

title: 5.9 magnitude earthquake hits Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, tremors felt in north India
content: A 5.9 magnitude earthquake originating in Afghanistan's Jurm jolted Delhi-NCR and northern India on Friday evening. Residents across Jammu and Kashmir, Delhi, and surrounding areas felt the tremors and evacuated their homes. Fortunately, no injuries or property damage have been reported thus far in this developing situation.

title: Reason the 6am email from 'Oracle leadership' told thousands of laid-off employees
content: Oracle has begun its largest-ever round of layoffs, with employees receiving termination emails from "Oracle Leadership" at 6AM saying their roles had been eliminated after "careful consideration of Oracle's current business needs." Around 10,000 employees have reportedly been cut so far, with estimates suggesting the total could reach 30,000. The email gave employees a narrow window to act before systems were deactivated.

title: 'Spinners' off day cost us': CSK captain points to Chahar, Noor after PBKS loss
content: "We felt we had three seamers going in, and obviously we have two good wrist spinners (Noor Ahmad and Rahul Chahar) bowling in tandem, especially in the middle after the powerplay. We felt that, you know, it will help," said Gaikwad. "But I think, you know, slight off day for both of them. So, I think, that is what cost us. Sometimes we executed really well. Sometimes we just didn't execute at the right time," he added.

title: MeT forecasts heavy rains in Kashmir plains, snowfall in higher reaches
content: Jammu and Kashmir braces for continued erratic weather as a strong western disturbance brings widespread rain, gusty winds, and snowfall to higher reaches. Srinagar experienced flooded roads, prompting authorities to issue advisories urging residents to avoid non-essential travel and take safety precautions, especially near water bodies. The inclement conditions are expected to persist until April 10.

title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has a message for tech CEOs blaming AI for layoffs
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff is pushing back on the narrative that AI is driving mass white-collar layoffs, calling it an oversimplification—and in some cases, a CEO's "lazy way out." While companies like Block and Atlassian have framed sweeping cuts as AI-driven restructuring, Benioff argues they're conflating three very different problems. At Salesforce, he's doing the opposite: hiring 20% more salespeople, convinced that human connection is something no AI can replicate.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 16: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 950 crore
content: In just 16 days, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has rewritten box office history, amassing a staggering Rs. 939.46 crore India net. The Aditya Dhar directorial, starring Ranveer Singh as the elite spy Hamza, has officially overtaken the lifetime collections of its predecessor in a mere 11 days. Despite the massive IPL 2026 season, the film continues to dominate theaters, crossing a global gross of Rs. 1,492.17 crore and becoming the highest-grossing Indian film of all time in North America. With powerful performances from Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, and Rakesh Bedi, this high-stakes espionage thriller is now rapidly closing in on the historic Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone.

title: 'Playing not to impress': Shreyas Iyer after CSK vs PBKS IPL match
content: Punjab Kings beat CSK by five wickets, chasing 210 with ease, led by Shreyas Iyer’s half-century. CSK posted 209/5, powered by Ayush Mhatre’s 73. PBKS got a flying start through Priyansh Arya and stayed composed. Iyer said the team aims to “impress each other,” highlighting strong starts and teamwork behind their top-of-the-table run.

title: Kerala elections SWOT analysis: Hat-trick for Vijayan or UDF comeback?
content: Kerala braces for a complex election as chief minister Pinarayi Vijayan seeks a rare third term. The traditional LDF-UDF rivalry faces a new challenge from the BJP's expanding footprint. With victories often decided by thousands of votes, this election hinges on micro-shifts and local dynamics, promising a tight, multi-layered contest.

title: Anthropic's doomsday warning to automation push as Claude leak blamed on human error
content: Anthropic's Claude Code creator Boris Cherny confirmed that a missed manual step in the deployment process caused the accidental release of 512,000 lines of internal source code. Nobody was fired. His fix: more automation, with Claude checking its own deploys. The irony isn't subtle—Cherny is the same person who predicted the software engineer job title would start disappearing in 2026, and coding was already "practically solved."

title: Gavaskar gives huge Green verdict: 'If he's not going to bowl ...'
content: Former India batter Sunil Gavaskar questioned Kolkata Knight Riders’ balance due to Cameron Green not bowling, suggesting a specialist batter may be needed. He also flagged middle-order concerns amid back-to-back losses. Meanwhile, Gavaskar praised Nitish Kumar Reddy’s improved pace and all-round performance for Sunrisers Hyderabad, calling it encouraging for India.

title: Madras HC denies Cognizant’s plea, says sudden firing, gate denial breach worker dignity
content: The court made the observation while refusing to allow an appeal moved by Cognizant Technologies Solutions India challenging an order passed by a labour appellate authority quashing illegal termination of an employee who served the company for 18 years.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for April 4, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 4, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Jalen Hurts linked to 4 trade spots amid Philadelphia Eagles offensive tensions
content: Speculation is mounting over Jalen Hurts’ future with his parent franchise Philadelphia Eagles. The reasons could be reported offensive friction and his resistance to scheme changes. Analysts suggest potential trade destinations including the Pittsburgh Steelers, Miami Dolphins, New York Jets, and Cleveland Browns, each presenting unique scenarios for the star quarterback.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry join $150b mogul in heartwarming move
content: Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry have joined Warren Buffett in reviving his iconic charity lunch through a new auction titled “A Seat At The Table.” The initiative blends Buffett’s legacy in philanthropy with the Currys’ growing influence beyond basketball. With proceeds benefiting the Glide Foundation and Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation, the event aims to create meaningful change in underserved communities. Scheduled for June 24 in Omaha, the auction reflects a powerful collaboration focused on impact, purpose, and long-term community support.

title: John Cena & The Undertaker reunion set for WrestleMania 42
content: WrestleMania 42 in Las Vegas will feature a major reunion as WWE legends The Undertaker and John Cena share the stage. Cena, who is also the official host for the two-night event, will join The Undertaker for a live, unscripted 'Six Feet Under' recording at WWE World. This highly anticipated fan event promises candid conversations between two iconic figures.

title: 'I am a Muslim': Lamine Yamal condemns 'racist' anti-Muslim chants vs Egypt
content: The incident took place at RCDE Stadium on Tuesday, where parts of the crowd were heard chanting: "bote, bote, bote musulman el que no bote", which translates to "jump, jump, jump, whoever doesn’t jump is a Muslim". The chant was heard again later in the first half and shortly after the break.

title: Bat-check controversy: How Mhatre failed thrice due to umpire error
content: A bizarre moment in the IPL 2026 match between Chennai Super Kings and Punjab Kings saw Ayush Mhatre’s bat fail multiple checks due to an upside-down gauge. After confusion and delays, the error was fixed and his original bat passed. The incident, though harmless, became a widely discussed and humorous highlight of the match.

title: Rajpal Yadav on Rs 9 cr debt row, says Salman, Akshay can’t do without him
content: Actor Rajpal Yadav, recently granted bail in a cheque bounce case, clarified he hasn't faced professional struggles. Speaking with Farah Khan, he revealed a prolific career, having starred in approximately 50 films each with Ram Gopal Varma, Priyadarshan, and David Dhawan, and over 200 films since 2000, emphasizing his consistent work.

title: 'You guys look great': Artemis II shares image of Earth from space
content: The Artemis II crew has shared stunning images of Earth from their historic lunar voyage, humanity's first in over 50 years. This 10-day test flight around the Moon is a crucial step for NASA's ambitious Artemis program, aiming for a lunar landing by 2028. Despite a minor toilet malfunction, the astronauts are proceeding with their mission.

title: Chennai crowd forces Bishop to say 'sorry' during toss - Watch
content: The IPL 2026 toss between Chennai Super Kings and Punjab Kings was disrupted by a roaring Chepauk crowd, forcing Ian Bishop to apologise for the delays. Shreyas Iyer chose to bowl first. Ruturaj Gaikwad explained CSK’s opening strategy and pitch conditions as they aim to recover from a poor start.

title: The desert is not neutral: How heat and sand change weapons in war
content: As Iran's drones and ballistic missiles rain down on Gulf cities, oil terminals and airfields, a quieter battle is being fought against sand, heat and open sky — one that has reshaped the outcome of every conflict ever fought on Arabian soil.

title: 'Hey DM, where is CID?’: AIMIM neta's video threatening Bengal SIR officer viral
content: AIMIM leader Mofakrul Islam was arrested for his alleged role in the Malda violence, where judicial officers were held hostage. The Supreme Court criticized the West Bengal administration's failure to maintain law and order, ordering a central probe by the NIA. The incident has heightened political tensions ahead of the West Bengal elections.

title: Russia plans to step-up energy supply to India – Will it offset Middle East volatility?
content: Russia is ready to boost oil and liquefied natural gas supplies to India. This comes as the Middle East crisis creates global energy market volatility. Discussions between Russian and Indian officials covered expanding trade, investment, and industrial cooperation. Both nations also explored deepening engagement in civil nuclear energy. Russia confirmed its capacity for steady increases in energy shipments to India.

title: 'Can easily open Hormuz, take oil & make fortune': Trump makes 'gushing' claims
content: Trump's remark is concurrent to Washington's repeated claims that talks with Tehran have been "progressing well", though the Islamic regime, currently without the leadership, has outrightly quashed the Trump's claim. Trump's fresh assertion on Hormuz, a key chokepoint in the global oil supply route, comes a day after he appeared disgruntled that Western allies, particularly NATO, refused to shoulder their share in the war, which is nearing its fifth week after a joint US-Israeli aerial assault.

title: 'Keep your head on your shoulders': Yuvraj warns Sooryavanshi
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, 15, is emerging as a cricket prodigy after record-breaking IPL and Under-19 performances. Inspired by Brian Lara and Yuvraj Singh, he aims to deliver strong starts for Rajasthan Royals. Yuvraj praised his exceptional bat speed and all-round power, saying his India debut is inevitable, “not if, but when”, while stressing the importance of staying grounded.

title: Tamil Nadu elections: Why Annamalai missed BJP candidate list - alliance over leader?
content: K Annamalai's absence from the BJP's candidate list for the Tamil Nadu assembly elections has sparked speculation. Despite being sidelined, Annamalai pledged to support all NDA candidates aiming for a significant victory. His removal as state president and subsequent marginalization are linked to alliance dynamics with AIADMK and caste considerations.

title: IPL 2026: Why is MS Dhoni not playing in CSK vs PBKS?
content: Chennai Super Kings are missing MS Dhoni due to a calf injury during their IPL 2026 clash with Punjab Kings, who chose to bowl first. After a poor opening loss, concerns remain, though coach Stephen Fleming said, “He is still heavily involved… his influence remains very strong.” CSK, led by Ruturaj Gaikwad, are rebuilding while also awaiting Dewald Brevis’ return from injury.

title: Five Indians among 12 injured by Iranian missile debris in Abu Dhabi
content: Debris from an intercepted Iranian missile injured at least 12 people in Abu Dhabi. Five Indian nationals were among those hurt. The incident occurred in the Ajban area. This follows rising tensions in the Gulf region. Churches in the UAE announced closures for Easter Sunday. Iran's Revolutionary Guard warned of attacks on US tech companies.

title: Bigger & quieter: What makes India's 3rd nuclear submarine INS Aridhaman deadly
content: India significantly bolstered its maritime defense with the commissioning of INS Aridaman, its third indigenous nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine. This strategic induction enhances the nation's nuclear triad capabilities. Additionally, the frigate Taragiri, a testament to 'Make in India' and advanced shipbuilding, was also inducted, boosting naval combat readiness and operational might.

title: Praggnanandhaa’s invisible second: The making of Vaibhav Suri
content: FIDE Master Prasenjit Dutta, a former prodigy from Tripura, runs a chess academy in New Delhi. He mentored Vaibhav Suri, India’s 27th Grandmaster, whose dedication and positional mastery shaped his rise. Today, Suri focuses entirely on supporting young phenom R Praggnanandhaa, applying his deep strategic insight as a coach, analyst, and mentor behind the scenes of elite international chess.

title: 'They look committed': Yuvraj’s direct message to Gambhir-Agarkar on Rohit-Kohli future
content: "I don’t know what’s going on in their minds. They look committed to playing the World Cup, but I think that decision lies with them and the selectors, who will have to decide the future. My point is that they are such great players, there has to be clarity," Yuvraj said.

title: Why first tanker of Iran oil to India in 7 yrs was diverted to China
content: A US-sanctioned tanker carrying Iranian crude oil, initially bound for India, has rerouted to China. Payment issues appear to be the primary driver for this change, as sellers are reportedly tightening credit terms. This development highlights the ongoing challenges in facilitating Iranian oil trade, even with temporary US sanctions waivers.

title: 'Fraud Czar': Trump gives JD Vance new role; 'Blue States' in crosshairs
content: President Trump has appointed Vice President JD Vance as "Fraud Czar" to investigate fraud in federal benefit programs, particularly in Democrat-run states. This initiative aims to combat widespread theft of taxpayer money and could potentially balance the American budget. Raids have reportedly begun in Los Angeles as part of this effort.

title: France no longer hostile nation? After Macron snubs Trump, Iran allows ship to pass Hormuz
content: A French-owned container ship, the Kribi, crossed the Strait of Hormuz, potentially signaling a shift in Iran's view of France following President Macron's criticism of US policy. Macron rejected military intervention to reopen the strait, advocating for negotiations instead. The vessel's change of destination to "Owner France" may have signaled its nationality to Iranian authorities.

title: Teachers’ federation plans to stage protests on April 18
content: APTF urges the government to constitute the 12th PRC with effect from July 2023, announce 30% interim relief and appoint a HM and a PET in every high school, irrespective of student strength

title: Tamil Nadu missed out on Central government schemes because DMK saw BJP as an enemy, says G.K. Vasan
content: This is an election for the safety of the people of Tamil Nadu, especially women. The DMK does not want any development from the Centre because of vote bank politics. It should be a double-engine sarkar; the Central and State governments should work together, says the TMC(M) president

title: When an unknown 29-year-old took on K. Kamaraj in 1962
content: Sixty-four years ago, P. Ramamoorthy, now 92, volunteered to fight against the then all-powerful Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu in Sattur, though he had nothing personal against him. The election did not generate any surprise. Kamaraj won hands down

title: Yadgir DC issues show-cause notices to two LPG agencies for violating rules
content: The agencies face charges of selling cylinders at a higher price than that fixed by the government to customers who had not even booked a cylinder, and caused inconvenience to genuine customers

title: Pinarayi Vijayan slams T.K. Govindan, backs P.K. Shyamala in Taliparamba
content: Mr. Vijayan alleged that Mr. Govindan had shifted his political stance purely out of seat ambitions, terming his move as an act that goes beyond mere breach of trust

title: The CPI(M) does not need to launch all leaders as MLAs or for other positions for live, says CPI(M) veteran M.M. Mani
content: M.M. Mani predicts a resounding third consecutive term for the LDF in the Assembly with over 100 seats

title: Honorary doctorates to politicians raises eyebrows
content: The two politicians are Congress MLC K. Govindaraj and B.M. Farooq, an industrialist from Mangaluru identified with the Janata Dal (Secular)

title: When welfare met demographic concerns
content: A new study revisits the parliamentary debates in the 1960s over linking maternity benefits to population control in India

title: MHA replaces ‘most affected’ tag with other LWE categories
content: Red Corridor shrinks to just two districts in 2026, reflecting a shift in the severity of Left Wing Extremism across affected regions

title: LDF corrected course after setback, confident of minority support, says RJD State secretary general Varughese George
content: The decision of senior leaders to step aside was voluntary and aimed at creating space for new candidates, says Varughese George

title: Death toll hits five in Bihar ‘hooch tragedy’, 12 arrested, SHO suspended
content: State Opposition leader Tejashwi Yadav slams the State government, saying the incident once again exposes the failure of the NDA government and its prohibition law.

title: Election petitions must be decided on the basis of material on record: Supreme Court
content: The order was based on an appeal challenging a February 2025 Punjab and Haryana High Court order on elections to the post of sarpanch of gram panchayat Khalila Majra at Panipat

title: Theorising a third term for LDF, one couplet at a time
content: CPI (M)’s M.A. Baby says the ‘alternative model’ led by the Left should not be disrupted; he says BJP’s communal politics is the biggest challenge for Kerala and that the Congress is yet to understand the threat of communal fascism

title: Russia promises to increase oil, LNG supplies to India
content: Russia says ‘particular attention’ paid to cooperation in oil and gas sector; supply of mineral fertilizers to India increased by 40%

title: Congress is politically incompetent and ideologically bankrupt, says D. Raja
content: The Left Democratic Front government in Kerala has been successful on many fronts and will win a third consecutive term, says the CPI general secretary; accusing the Congress of resorting to ‘slanderous campaigns and allegations’, he says it must criticise the government on the basis of issues

title: Minister Payyavula Keshav invites Arvind Limited to set up garment units in Anantapur
content: The Finance Minister and the firm’s representatives discuss investment prospects and opportunities in Anantapur region

title: Watch: G.K. Vasan: Tamil Nadu missed central schemes as DMK treated BJP as ‘enemy’
content: GK Vasan, president of the Tamil Maanila Congress (Moopanar), speaks about the upcoming Assembly elections in Tamil Nadu. He talks about his party’s alliance with the BJP, why the country has rejected the Congress party and that the priority of the State should be safety, especially for women.

title: Congress calls delimitation move ‘dangerous,’ says women’s law amended to grab political mileage in polls
content: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge and LoP in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi to meet party colleagues and other Opposition leaders to strategise for the special session, says Jairam Ramesh, adding the move to amend women’s quota law in the middle of elections violates model code

title: India keeps plastic waste targets intact, but eases path to compliance
content: New provisions allow companies to carry forward the shortfall in meeting targets and formalise a system of tradable certificates

title: Habitual offender arrested in Nandyal, gold and cash seized
content: The accused was wanted in multiple offences reported from Nandyal, Bandi Atmakur, Revanur, Sanjamala, Gadivemula, and other parts of Rayalaseema, say police

title: Telangana ACB uncovers ₹42 lakh in suspicious transactions at Warangal Sub-Registrar Office; recovers 819 grams gold, 2.6 kg silver from residences of two SROs’
content: As many as 20 unauthorised agents and document writers were found inside Sub-Registrar Office premises

title: Bus goes up in flames after hit by two wheeler
content: None of the bus passengers was reported hurt. However, the motorcyclist who collided with the bus was killed on the spot

title: Betting buzz: Men's Final Four features tightest spreads on record
content: our betting buzz file aims to provide fans a look at the sports betting stories that are driving the conversation . the men's final four will feature two of the tightest spreads in its history when it gets underway . it would be the first time since the tournament expanded to 64 teams in 1985 that both national semifinals had spreads of two or less .

title: 117 wins? 40 different pitchers? Four themes that will dominate the Dodgers' regular season
content: the identity of the 2026 los Angeles Dodgers is presented in a 100-second hype video . it's set to the beat of Billie Eilish's "Bad Guy" and begins with a question, asked by actor Jason Bateman . "you need teams like us so you can point your finger and say, "That's the bad guy," he says .

title: Final Four has a Carolina blue backdrop
content: it's been over a week since north Carolina fired coach Hubert Davis . there have been few updates on the north Carolina Tar Heels' coaching search . despite a dynamic men's final four in Indianapolis this weekend, UNC will be a primary topic of discussion .

title: What Neil Armstrong really took from the Moon during the Apollo 11 mission
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. title: Konnor Griffin is headed to the majors! What you need to know about Pirates' 19-year-old phenom
content: the Pittsburgh Pirates are calling up the top prospect in the sport . the 19-year-old shortstop is expected to become MLB's first teenage position player since Juan Soto debuted in 2018 . he is the best prospect baseball has seen in years .

title: Draft prospects with true Round 1 grade: The 12 clear first-rounders
content: the average draft class has roughly 15 players who are deemed "first-round talents" a player would have been a first-rounder in each of the past five classes . the 2026 class ended with fewer players -- only 12 .

title: NHL Power Rankings: New 1-32 poll, most important remaining games
content: there are fewer than 14 days left in the 2025-26 NHL regular season . each team has more than 10 games remaining . there are specific matchups that will have an outsized impact on the playoff races (and the draft lottery standings)

title: Power Rankings: Who are the top teams one week into the season?
content: rookies have had stellar starts to their major league careers . no teams have gone undefeated in the first week of game play . we've also seen bullpen concerns in the early days . a team atop the standings in the u.s. has a stellar start .

title: 'Let her play': Chiefs launch petition for high school girls' flag football in Kansas
content: the Kansas City Chiefs announced an advertising campaign on friday . the campaign is aimed at persuading the KSHSAA to make girls' flag football a sanctioned sport in the state . in a 55-second video, the Chiefs showcased several girls who already play the sport .

title: Amid UNC talk, Lloyd agrees to new deal at Zona
content: Tommy Lloyd agrees to a new five-year deal with the school . the 51-year-old says he's staying at Arizona . he said he made a decision that his Michael Jordan is Steve Kerr .

title: Griffin joins Skenes, Ohtani on list of hyped MLB debuts
content: Konnor Griffin will make his major league debut against the Baltimore Orioles on friday . the 19-year-old is set to be the first player to make his MLB debut as a teenager since 2018 . he is the first teenager to suit up for the Pirates since 1998 .

title: Why Hormuz blockade brings back memories of 1956 Suez crisis
content: the crisis in Suez serves as a lifeline for global trade by facilitating the shortest shipping route between Asia and Europe and America . the conflict was triggered by then Egyptian president Gamal Abdel Nasser’s decision to nationalise the Anglo-French company operating the Canal . but the power equations in West Asia in 1956 were starkly different from those that were to play out in the theatre of war in the same region seventy years later .
